---
title: "RPP FFT API Introduction"
subtitle: "rppfft Interface Guide and Training Deck"
author: "XDL Technical Support"
date: "2026-01-18"
lang: en

format:
  revealjs:
    theme: [league, xdl-tech-theme.scss]
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">
        <link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin>
        <link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=IBM+Plex+Mono:wght@400;500;600&family=IBM+Plex+Sans:wght@400;500;600;700&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
    slide-number: true
    chalkboard: false
    preview-links: auto
    small-footer: "XDL RPP"
    transition: slide
    background-transition: fade
    width: 1280
    height: 720
  pptx:
    theme: simple
    slide-number: true
    footer: "XDL RPP"
execute:
  echo: false
code-fold: false
logo: pic/logo_color_horizontal.png
reference-doc: template_xdl3.pptx
---

## Scope and Goal

- Goal: understand core `rppfft` APIs and recommended parameter patterns
- Scope: API walkthrough, fast/normal path, stream usage, and practical constraints
- Audience: developers with basic RPP runtime and C/C++ experience



## RPP FFT Overview

`rppfft` is the FFT library on RPP platform. Typical capabilities:

- 1D and 2D transforms
- Complex-to-Complex floating-point FFT
- Stream-based asynchronous execution

- Headers: `rppfft.h`, `fft_com.h`
- Core flow: Plan -> (optional) SetStream -> Exec -> Destroy



## Constants and Types (`rppfft.h`)

| Name | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `RPPFFT_FORWARD` | Forward transform |
| `RPPFFT_INVERSE` | Inverse transform |
| `RPPFFT_DIR_INIT` | Direction unspecified at plan creation |
| `RPPFFT_C2C` | Complex-to-Complex FFT |

Return type: `rppfftResult`

| Value | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `RPPFFT_SUCCESS` | Success |
| `RPPFFT_INVALID_VALUE` | Invalid parameter |
| `RPPFFT_NOT_SUPPORTED` | Unsupported `nx` / `batch` |
| `RPPFFT_INVALID_PLAN` | Invalid plan (for example locked/illegal state) |
| `RPPFFT_ALLOC_FAILED` | GPU resource allocation failed |

Data type: `rppfftComplex` (`.x` real, `.y` imag).



## 1D Plan API: `rppfftPlan1d`

```cpp
rppfftResult rppfftPlan1d(rppfftHandle *plan,
    int nx,
    rppfftType type,
    int batch,
    bool isShift,
    bool columnFft,
    bool fastAlgo,
    rppfftComplex *idata,
    rppfftComplex *odata,
    int direction,
    rpp_graph_mgr::GraphManager *graphmgr);
```

- Runtime I/O mode: pass `NULL` for `idata` / `odata`, then set buffers at exec time
- Fixed I/O mode: bind `device_in`, `device_out`, and direction during plan creation

Inputs: plan pointer, `nx`, `type`, `batch`, `isShift`, `columnFft`, `fastAlgo`, optional I/O/direction  
Output: `plan` becomes a valid executable handle



## 2D Plan API: `rppfftPlan2d`

```cpp
rppfftResult rppfftPlan2d(rppfftHandle *plan,
    int nx, int ny,
    rppfftType type,
    bool fastAlgo,
    rppfftComplex *idata,
    rppfftComplex *odata,
    int direction,
    rpp_graph_mgr::GraphManager *graphmgr);
```

- 2D API does not include `batch`, `isShift`, or `columnFft`
- Same concepts as 1D for `fastAlgo`, fixed/runtime I/O, and direction



## `rppfftExecC2C`

```cpp
rppfftResult rppfftExecC2C(rppfftHandle plan,
    rppfftComplex *idata, rppfftComplex *odata, int direction);
```

- Inputs: plan, device input/output pointers, transform direction
- Output: frequency-domain data in `odata`
- In-place mode: if `idata == odata`, transform runs in-place



## `rppfftSetStream` / `rppfftDestroy`

- `rppfftSetStream`: bind plan to a runtime stream; FFT kernels launch on this stream and can overlap with transfer streams
- Stream must come from `rtStreamCreate()`; `0` means default stream
- `rppfftDestroy`: release plan resources and internal structures; must be called to avoid leaks



## Minimal 1D Example

```cpp
#define NX 256
#define BATCH 32
rppfftHandle plan;
rppfftComplex *idata, *odata;
rtMalloc((void**)&idata, sizeof(rppfftComplex)*NX*BATCH);
rtMalloc((void**)&odata, sizeof(rppfftComplex)*NX*BATCH);
rppfftPlan1d(&plan, NX, RPPFFT_C2C, BATCH, false, true, false, NULL, NULL, RPPFFT_DIR_INIT, nullptr);
rppfftSetStream(plan, stream);
rppfftExecC2C(plan, idata, odata, RPPFFT_FORWARD);
rtStreamSynchronize(stream);
rppfftDestroy(plan);
rtFree(idata);
rtFree(odata);
```



## Fast vs Normal Path

| Mode | Data Path | When to Use |
|---|---|---|
| **Fast** (`fastAlgo=true`) | internal tensors remain in SRAM (no DDR spill) | typically when `batch * nx <= 512K` |
| **Normal** (`fastAlgo=false`) | direct DDR-buffer path | large-scale workloads or no SRAM acceleration needed |

Typical Fast-path benefit: around 2x in suitable workloads (depends on shape and pipeline).



## Fast Path Example (1D)

```cpp
// 1) SRAM allocation and preload
smgr.Allocate(device_in, batch, length, sizeof(rppfftComplex), HCFMT);
smgr.Allocate(device_out, batch, length, sizeof(rppfftComplex), RCFMT);
smgr.Download(device_in);
smgr.ReformatInPlace(device_in);

// 2) Plan creation (Fixed I/O example)
rppfftPlan1d(&plan, length, RPPFFT_C2C, batch, isShift, columnFft, true,
             device_in, device_out, direction);

// 3) Execute
rppfftExecC2C(plan, device_in, device_out, direction);

// 4) Upload output to DDR
smgr.ReformatInPlace(device_out);
smgr.Upload(device_out);
```



## Format Notes: `HCFMT` / `RCFMT`

For complex FFT data, common format pairs are:

- `HCFMT`: host-friendly complex layout
- `RCFMT`: RPP-compute-friendly complex layout

In Fast path, Reformat is often required before/after compute so both operator requirements and host-side readability are satisfied.



## Runtime I/O vs Fixed I/O

| Mode | During Plan | During Exec |
|---|---|---|
| **Runtime I/O** | `idata`/`odata` are `NULL` | each exec can change buffer and direction |
| **Fixed I/O** | bind `device_in`/`device_out` at plan time | exec uses the same buffers |

Performance note: if plan-time and exec-time I/O buffers are consistent, a typical extra gain around 20% is possible.



## `columnFft` and `isShift`

`columnFft` affects layout interpretation and scheduling:

| `columnFft` | Meaning | Performance |
|---|---|---|
| `true` | column-direction FFT, Batch Major input | often around +20% |
| `false` | row-direction FFT, Nx Major input, may require internal transpose | depends on shape |

`isShift` controls whether fftshift is applied after FFT.



## `isShift` / fftshift Explained

Issue: default FFT output places DC (zero frequency) at index 0.

`fftshift` swaps spectral halves so DC moves to center.

1D example (length 8):

```text
Original FFT output: [DC, f1, f2, f3, f4, f5, f6, f7]
After fftshift:      [f4, f5, f6, f7, DC, f1, f2, f3]
                                      ^
                                  DC centered
```

Common use: spectrum visualization, filtering, image processing; disable with `isShift=false` if not needed.



## Plan vs Exec

| API | Role | Call Timing |
|---|---|---|
| `rppfftPlan1d` | build plan: resource allocation, twiddle setup, algorithm config | once per FFT configuration |
| `rppfftExecC2C` | execute FFT on current buffers using existing plan | every run, repeatable |

Analogy: Plan = prepare tool; Exec = use tool on concrete data.

One plan can run many exec calls if shape/config stays the same.



## 2D / 3D API Status

- 2D: supported by `rppfftPlan2d`
- 3D: TBD (follow latest User Guide status)



## 2D Example (Demo Extract)

```cpp
rppfftPlan2d(&plan, height, width, RPPFFT_C2C, fastAlgo,
             device_in, device_out, direction);  // or NULL,NULL,RPPFFT_DIR_INIT
rppfftSetStream(plan, stream);
rppfftExecC2C(plan, device_in, device_out, direction);
// For Fast path, add SRAM Allocate/Download/Reformat/Upload around Exec
rppfftDestroy(plan);
```



## Common Parameter Combinations (Training)

| Scenario | batch | length | columnFft | fastAlgo | runtimeIo |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1D column FFT fast path | 1024 | 256 | true | true | false |
| 1D row FFT fast path | 1024 | 512 | false | true | true |
| 1D large normal path | 1024 | 8192 | true | false | false |
| 2D fast path | — | 1024x128 | — | true | true |



## Error Handling and Constraints

- Always check `ret == RPPFFT_SUCCESS` after plan APIs
- Before Fast path, call `smgr.Init(SRAM_WORK_SPACE)` (for example from `fft_com.h`)
- In Fast mode, after exec: `ReformatInPlace(device_out)` then `Upload(device_out)`
- Reuse one plan for multiple exec calls; always call `rppfftDestroy` at end


## Common Constants in `fft_com.h`

| Constant | Meaning |
|---|---|
| `SRAM_WORK_SPACE` | SRamManager workspace size for Fast path (for example around 23MB) |
| `FAST_FFT_SIZE` | maximum FFT shape supported by Fast algorithm (for example `1024x512`) |

Before Fast path, initialize SRAM manager with `smgr.Init(SRAM_WORK_SPACE)` (or equivalent workspace size).



## Build and Link

Header path: `/usr/local/rpp/include` (`rppfft.h`)

```bash
mkdir -p build
cd build
cmake ..
make -j
```

If compiling manually, ensure RPP include/link paths and library linkage are correctly configured.



## Summary

- APIs: `rppfftPlan1d` / `rppfftPlan2d`, `rppfftExecC2C`, `rppfftSetStream`, `rppfftDestroy`
- Typical performance guidance: Fast (`batch*nx <= 512K`) around 2x; `columnFft=true` often adds ~20%; Fixed I/O can add ~20%
